title: Small Business Lending Fund
summary: The Small Business Lending Fund (SBLF) was established by the Small Business Jobs Act of 2010 to encourage community bank lending to small businesses, with Treasury investing over $4.0 billion across 332 institutions. As of Q3 2025, the cumulative net impact on qualified small business lending over baseline is $19.1 billion, and 327 of 332 institutions have fully redeemed their Treasury investments as of December 1, 2025.
key_points: SBLF was established by the Small Business Jobs Act of 2010 to provide capital to community banks and community development loan funds
Treasury invested $4.0 billion total: $3.9 billion in 281 community banks and $104 million in 51 CDLFs across 47 states and DC
Participating institutions operate in over 3,000 locations across the United States
As of Q3 2025, total cumulative net impact on qualified small business lending is $19.1 billion above baseline
Baseline is defined as the average of the four calendar quarters ended June 30, 2010
327 institutions with $3.95 billion in aggregate investments have fully redeemed and exited the program as of December 1, 2025
1 institution has partially redeemed $3.5 million (70% of its SBLF securities) while remaining in the program
why_it_matters: This is historical program information rather than a current macro catalyst. The SBLF is a completed policy initiative from 2010 where most participating institutions have already exited the program after redeeming their Treasury investments. The data provides retrospective evidence on the program's effectiveness at stimulating small business lending, but does not represent current economic data or policy decisions that would affect markets today.
important_numbers: $19.1 billion cumulative net impact on QSBL (as of Q3 2025)

The SBLF has largely achieved its purpose, with 327 of 332 institutions having fully redeemed their Treasury investments. While the $19.1 billion cumulative lending impact is notable, this is retrospective program evaluation data. Not suitable for real-time market or macro catalyst tracking unless the archive is designed to include historical policy context.
